stories.title: PERKESO LINDUNG 24/7 — Implement Employee Second-Category Contribution Deduction Engine and Gazette-Rate Activation Workflow
stories.description: The Employees' Social Security (Amendment) Bill 2025 was passed by Parliament on 2 December 2025, introducing the LINDUNG 24/7 Non-Occupational Accident Scheme. A fundamental structural change is that BOTH employers AND employees must now contribute under Second Category — previously only employers contributed to Second Category (Invalidity Scheme). Contribution rates and effective date are pending PERKESO gazette announcement expected Q1/Q2 2026. All payroll systems must add the new employee-side Second Category deduction and update PERKESO ASSIST submission data once rates are officially gazetted.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'LINDUNG 24/7 Employee Contribution Rate' configuration field on PERKESO/SOCSO Settings with a 'Pending Gazette' status flag and activation date
When rate is entered and activation date reached, payroll engine automatically computes and deducts employee LINDUNG 24/7 contribution from gross pay per the gazette rate
Payslip displays new line item 'LINDUNG 24/7 (Employee)' as a separate deduction, distinct from existing First Category employee contribution
Employer LINDUNG 24/7 contribution amount added to employer cost calculation
PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman submission updated to include new LINDUNG 24/7 contribution amounts for both employer and employee per employee
System alert sent to HR Manager when gazette rate is entered, prompting payroll recalculation from the gazette effective date
Pre-gazette payroll runs display a compliance warning: 'LINDUNG 24/7 rates not yet gazetted — deduction not applied'
technicalNotes: Parliament passed amendment on 2 December 2025; contribution rates TBD via separate gazette announcement

Currently Second Category is employer-only (Invalidity Scheme); new LINDUNG 24/7 requires both employer+employee contribution — materially different from the label-only rebranding covered in existing LINDUNG story
PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman CSV/API may need new contribution code/column for LINDUNG 24/7 scheme — monitor perkeso.gov.my/en/rate-of-contribution.html
Expected implementation Q1 or at latest Q2 2026 per PERKESO announcement
Related: existing 'PERKESO LINDUNG Scheme Rebranding' story covers UI labels only; this story covers actual contribution computation and payslip deduction

stories.title: PERKESO LINDUNG 24/7 — Update e-Caruman Submission File with New Third-Scheme Contribution Code
stories.description: The PERKESO LINDUNG 24/7 amendment introduces a new protection scheme distinct from First Category (Employment Injury) and Second Category (Invalidity). When rates are gazetted, the PERKESO ASSIST e-Caruman electronic submission file will require a new contribution code for LINDUNG 24/7 amounts from both employer and employee. The existing e-Caruman generator handles only First and Second Category codes and must be extended for the new scheme without breaking existing submissions.
acceptanceCriteria: e-Caruman CSV/upload generator updated to include new LINDUNG 24/7 contribution code column for both employer and employee portions
Pre-gazette: LINDUNG 24/7 column exists but outputs zero / is flagged 'Pending Gazette' — file still passes PERKESO ASSIST validation
Post-gazette activation: LINDUNG 24/7 employer and employee contribution amounts correctly populated and accepted by PERKESO ASSIST upload
Historical e-Caruman files for periods before gazette effective date do not include LINDUNG 24/7 values, maintaining backward compatibility
Contribution receipt from PERKESO displays LINDUNG 24/7 amounts as a separate line in the system audit record
PERKESO ASSIST API upload response parsed to confirm new scheme fields accepted without rejection error
technicalNotes: Monitor PERKESO ASSIST developer portal for updated file format specification once LINDUNG 24/7 is gazetted
May require new column in e-Caruman CSV (e.g., Third_Category_Employer, Third_Category_Employee) or new XML element
Existing e-Caruman generation code lives in lhdn_payroll_integration/lhdn_payroll_integration/services/ — extend without breaking current First/Second Category columns
Rate announcement expected Q1/Q2 2026 at https://perkeso.gov.my/en/our-services/employer-employee/kadar-caruman.html
dependencies: PERKESO LINDUNG 24/7 — Implement Employee Second-Category Contribution Deduction Engine and Gazette-Rate Activation Workflow
Generate SOCSO and EIS e-Caruman Upload File (PERKESO ASSIST Portal)

stories.title: MyInvois RM10,000 Non-Consolidatable Transaction Rule — Route Large Payroll Expense Reimbursements to Individual Self-Billed e-Invoice
stories.description: Effective 1 January 2026, LHDN MyInvois rules prohibit consolidating any single transaction exceeding RM10,000 into a consolidated e-invoice. Payroll self-billed e-invoices for large employee expense reimbursements, relocation allowances, one-off perquisites, or BIK items exceeding RM10,000 per transaction must be issued as individual (non-consolidated) self-billed e-invoices with real-time MyInvois validation. The existing self-billed expense claim story does not address this amount-based routing split between consolidated and individual e-invoice workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: On Expense Claim or payroll perquisite generation, system checks if the individual transaction amount exceeds RM10,000
Transactions > RM10,000 are routed exclusively to individual self-billed e-invoice workflow, excluded from consolidated monthly batch
Transactions ≤ RM10,000 remain eligible for monthly consolidated self-billed e-invoice as before
HR/payroll user receives a clear warning when a claim exceeds the RM10,000 threshold before batch consolidation is executed
Audit log records the routing decision (individual vs consolidated) and reason (RM10,000 amount threshold) for each payroll expense transaction
Individual self-billed e-invoices for large amounts submitted to MyInvois API with all required invoice-level fields and receive an individual UUID from MyInvois
technicalNotes: RM10,000 threshold applies per single transaction, not per aggregate employee per month
Most affected payroll items: large travel reimbursements, relocation packages, one-off medical claims, ex-gratia payments classified as perquisites
The consolidated e-invoice limit for sub-RM10,000 items is unaffected — only the routing logic changes above the threshold
LHDN clarified effective date is 1 January 2026; Phase 4 companies have a grace period until 31 December 2026 for penalties
Reference: MyInvois SDK v2.1 for individual invoice vs consolidated invoice schema differences
dependencies: Self-Billed e-Invoice for Employee Expense Claims and Perquisite Reimbursements (MyInvois Phase 4)

stories.title: Gig Workers Act 872 — Enforce PERKESO Deduction on Separate Ministerial Gazette Enforcement Date, Not Royal Assent
stories.description: The Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872) received Royal Assent on 16 December 2025 and was gazetted on 31 December 2025. However, the Act explicitly states enforcement commences on a date separately determined by the Minister via gazette notification. As of March 2026, stakeholder engagement is still intensifying and enforcement has not started. The existing PRD story activates PERKESO deductions 'on Royal Assent', which is technically premature. A configurable enforcement-date gate must control when the 1.25% PERKESO deduction begins, preventing incorrect premature deductions from gig worker earnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Company-level configuration field 'Gig Workers Act 872 Enforcement Date' — initially empty/disabled
PERKESO LINDUNG KENDIRI deductions (1.25% per transaction) are NOT triggered until the enforcement date field is populated and the date is reached
System status panel shows: 'Act 872: Gazetted 31 Dec 2025 | Enforcement Date: PENDING MINISTERIAL GAZETTE'
When HR Admin enters the official ministerial enforcement date, system schedules deduction activation from that date
Alert sent to HR Admin 30 days and 7 days before enforcement date once entered
All platform-provider gig worker payment processing displays 'Pre-Enforcement — No PERKESO Deduction' until activation
If enforcement date is entered retroactively, system flags affected prior payroll periods for retroactive review
technicalNotes: Act 872 Section 1(2): 'This Act comes into operation on a date to be appointed by the Minister by notification in the Gazette'

Existing story 'Gig Workers Act 2025 — Platform Provider Registration as PERKESO Contributing Entity with Mandatory 1.25% LINDUNG KENDIRI Deduction on Royal Assent' has incorrect activation trigger — this gate must be applied
SOCSO target contribution rate: 1.25% of earnings per ride/delivery transaction
Monitor kesuma.gov.my and perkeso.gov.my for ministerial enforcement gazette notification
As of March 2026 gig worker talks are intensifying — enforcement date still unannounced per theedgemalaysia.com
dependencies: Gig Workers Act 2025 — Platform Provider Registration as PERKESO Contributing Entity with Mandatory 1.25% LINDUNG KENDIRI Deduction on Royal Assent

stories.title: TP1(1/2026) Domestic Tourism and Cultural Attraction Relief — YA2026-Only Expiry Guard and Auto-Deactivation
stories.description: Budget 2026 introduces a RM1,000 tax relief for entrance fees to domestic tourist attractions and cultural programs — explicitly for Year of Assessment 2026 only. Without a hard expiry guard, this temporary relief may be incorrectly applied in YA2027 payroll runs, causing over-deduction of PCB and potential LHDN audit exposure. A time-bounded eligibility flag with automatic deactivation from January 2027 prevents carry-forward errors and ensures PCB is compliant for both YA2026 and YA2027 simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Domestic tourism/cultural attraction relief line item on TP1 form is active only for payroll PCB periods January 2026 – December 2026
System automatically disables the relief input field from 1 January 2027 with expiry notice: 'Domestic Tourism Relief (YA2026 only) — Expired'
Existing employee TP1 declarations with this relief generate a HR alert when YA transitions to 2027: 'YA2026 temporary relief has expired — PCB will increase from January 2027'
EA Form template for YA2026 correctly includes the relief; YA2027 EA Form template excludes it
PCB recalculation for retroactive YA2026 adjustments correctly applies the time-bound eligibility; YA2027 recalculations ignore this relief item
Audit log shows 'Domestic Tourism Relief: Applied (YA2026 only)' or 'Not Applied (outside YA boundary)' per PCB computation
technicalNotes: Budget 2026 announcement specifies 'YA 2026' only — this is a time-limited relief, not permanent
Similar time-bound pattern to EV Charging relief (once per 2-YA biennial restriction) and PRS relief extension to YA2030
LHDN official TP1(1/2026) explanatory notes: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/i1lcd0wn/bi-explanatory-notes-for-from-tp1-2026.pdf
The existing story 'TP1(1/2026): Capture New Domestic Tourism Attraction Expenses Relief (RM1,000)' adds the field — this story adds the YA-expiry enforcement layer
dependencies: TP1(1/2026): Capture New Domestic Tourism Attraction Expenses Relief (RM1,000) in Employee PCB Declaration

stories.title: Malaysia Minimum Wage NWCC Biannual Review — Configurable Rate Registry with Gazette History and Bulk Below-Minimum Alert
stories.description: The National Wages Consultative Council (NWCC) reviews minimum wage every two years. The current RM1,700 rate (universal from August 2025) will be reviewed again in 2026/2027. The system currently treats RM1,700 as a hardcoded constant. A configurable minimum wage rate parameter with gazette history, effective-date versioning, and a bulk below-minimum alert ensures the system adapts to future NWCC gazette announcements without code changes — preventing any pay period where employees are underpaid against the new minimum.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimum Wage configuration DocType with fields: Rate (RM/month), Effective Date, Gazette Number, Gazette Date, Status (Active / Superseded)
Rate change history log: all previous minimum wage rates and their effective dates preserved and non-deletable for audit
On minimum wage rate creation/update, system scans all active employee salary records and flags any basic salary below the new rate
Bulk action: 'Apply Minimum Wage Update' to set all flagged employees to the new minimum with confirmation and audit log
HR dashboard shows: current active rate, gazette date, and an 'NWCC Review Advisory' when within 6 months of the 2-year anniversary of the current rate
Payroll pre-validation check blocks salary slip submission if any employee's computed gross is below current minimum wage rate
Penalty calculator auto-updates fine quantum calculations when the rate is changed in the registry
technicalNotes: Current active rate: RM1,700/month, effective universally from 1 August 2025 (small employers), 1 February 2025 (5+ employees)
Previous rate: RM1,500 effective 1 May 2022
NWCC statutory review cycle: at minimum every 2 years under Minimum Wages Order framework — next review expected 2026/2027
Monitor mohr.gov.my gazette page for NWCC announcement
Existing stories 'Enforce RM1,700 Minimum Wage for ALL Employers' cover enforcement rules; this story adds the configurable rate infrastructure layer
dependencies: Enforce RM1,700 Minimum Wage for ALL Employers Effective 1 August 2025
Minimum Wage Enforcement — Penalty Quantum Awareness Panel and Ongoing Violation Daily Fine Tracker

stories.title: FWCMS Multi-Tier Levy — Employer Tier Reclassification Trigger on Foreign Worker Headcount Change
stories.description: The FWCMS Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) charges different levy rates based on the ratio of foreign workers to total workforce. When an employer hires or terminates foreign workers, their MTLM tier classification may change — altering the levy rate for ALL currently employed foreign workers from the event date. The existing MTLM implementation calculates levy per worker at a static rate but does not trigger a tier-reclassification review when foreign worker headcount changes, risking under- or over-payment of levy at the wrong tier rate.
acceptanceCriteria: On creation or termination of any Foreign Worker employee record, system evaluates whether the new foreign-to-total-worker ratio crosses a configured MTLM tier boundary
If tier changes, HR Manager receives alert: 'Foreign worker ratio changed — FWCMS levy tier updated from Tier X to Tier Y effective [date]'
Retroactive recalculation option: recalculate all outstanding FWCMS levy amounts from tier-change date if new tier rate differs from previously applied rate
FWCMS Tier Status panel on Company record: current ratio, current tier, active levy rate, and headcount distance to next tier threshold
Audit log of all tier reclassification events with: trigger event (hire/termination), before/after tier, before/after levy rate, effective date
Monthly FWCMS levy report recalculates using the tier active for each sub-period within the month if a tier change occurred mid-month (pro-rated)
technicalNotes: MTLM tiers based on ratio of foreign workers to total local (Malaysian) workers — sector-specific thresholds apply (manufacturing differs from services and hospitality)

Existing story 'Implement Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) Rate Calculation for Foreign Workers' covers per-worker rate application at a static tier; this story covers the dynamic tier-change trigger on headcount events
Tier boundary thresholds: consult sector-specific FWCMS levy tables at fwcms.gov.my or mohr.gov.my
A mid-month tier change requires pro-rated levy: old tier rate for days before change date, new tier rate for days from change date onward
dependencies: Implement Multi-Tier Levy Model (MTLM) Rate Calculation for Foreign Workers
Record FWCMS Monthly Levy Payment Receipt Reference on Foreign Employee Record

stories.title: e-PCB Plus Monthly Employer Submission Batch Status Dashboard — Track Per-Employee Submission State per Payroll Period
stories.description: The e-PCB Plus system (live from January 2025) replaced e-Data PCB, e-PCB, and e-CP39 in a single MyTax portal. Employers submit monthly PCB for all employees, but the current integration only handles individual salary-slip-level acknowledgement storage. There is no consolidated employer-level view showing which employees have been submitted for a given payroll period, which are pending, and whether the batch payment to LHDN has been made. This gap risks individual employees being missed in monthly PCB remittance — an ITA Section 107A penalty exposure.
acceptanceCriteria: Monthly e-PCB Plus Batch Status view listing all employees in a payroll period with per-employee status: Draft | Submitted | Payment Pending | Payment Confirmed | Rejected
One-click 'Submit All Draft' batch action to submit all pending employees to e-PCB Plus in a single operation
FPX/JomPay payment reference number field recorded against the employer batch after LHDN payment is made
Alert if any active employee in a completed payroll run has not been submitted to e-PCB Plus by the 15th of the following month
Batch total PCB amount displayed alongside sum of individual salary slip PCB deductions — any discrepancy highlighted
Drill-down from batch view to individual salary slip e-PCB Plus acknowledgement reference number
technicalNotes: e-PCB Plus live from January 2025 — employers must have registered PCB Administrator on MyTax portal first
Submission deadline: 15th of the following month (e.g., January payroll due by 15 February)

Existing story 'Build Monthly e-PCB Plus Submission Reconciliation View' focuses on PCB amount reconciliation; this story focuses on per-employee submission tracking and batch payment confirmation status
Existing story 'e-PCB Plus Successful Submission — Store Acknowledgement Reference Number on Salary Slip' covers per-slip ack; this covers the month-level employer batch view
Both individual CP39 and bulk data upload flows must be reflected in the batch status
dependencies: Implement e-CP39 API Submission to LHDN MyTax / e-PCB Plus
Build Monthly e-PCB Plus Submission Reconciliation View
e-PCB Plus Successful Submission — Store Acknowledgement Reference Number on Salary Slip for LHDN Audit Trail

stories.title: EIS S.45F MYFutureJobs Job Vacancy Notification — Pre-Build Portal API Integration for Post-Moratorium Readiness
stories.description: The EIS (Amendment) Bill 2025 Section 45F requires employers to notify PERKESO via the MYFutureJobs portal when a vacancy is filled, within 7 days — penalty up to RM10,000 per violation. A 2-year moratorium is in effect from December 2025. The existing PRD story covers moratorium suppression only. This story builds the actual MYFutureJobs API submission workflow, notification reference number storage, and 7-day deadline tracker so the system is immediately compliant the moment the moratorium expires — with zero deployment lag.
acceptanceCriteria: Job Opening DocType gains new fields: 'S.45F MYFutureJobs Notification Status' (Draft/Suppressed-Moratorium/Submitted/Overdue) and 'PERKESO Notification Reference Number'
When a Job Opening is marked Filled (Employee record linked), system creates a pending S.45F notification task
During active moratorium: task status is set to 'Suppressed — Moratorium Active' and not submitted to MYFutureJobs
Moratorium end-date configuration field; once moratorium expires, newly filled jobs auto-trigger with 7-day submission deadline
Overdue alert fires if a filled vacancy exceeds 7 days without a PERKESO reference number after moratorium ends
HR compliance report shows all filled jobs in the past 30 days with moratorium/submission status, reference numbers, and overdue count
technicalNotes: MYFutureJobs portal: myfuturejobs.gov.my — verify if employer API or digital form submission is available for S.45F notifications
S.45F notification must be 'in writing' to SOCSO — may be satisfied by MYFutureJobs portal digital submission
Moratorium duration: up to 2 years from Bill passage (December 2025) — estimated moratorium end: December 2027
Fine: not exceeding RM10,000 per violation on conviction
SMEs were given extra time per PERKESO December 2025 announcement — moratorium phase is indefinitely in effect until gazette
dependencies: EIS Amendment 2025 S.45F — Moratorium Phase Suppression and Voluntary Job Vacancy Reporting Module

stories.title: Malaysia Gig Workers Act 872 — i-Saraan Plus Government Matching Contribution Report for Platform Providers
stories.description: Budget 2026 introduced i-Saraan Plus, offering gig workers government EPF matching of up to RM600 per year (RM6,000 lifetime), an improvement over i-Saraan's RM500/year. Platform providers who register gig workers as EPF members and facilitate voluntary i-Saraan Plus deductions from earnings must report contribution amounts to KWSP monthly. This is distinct from mandatory EPF contributions for regular employees. The existing 'Implement Gig Worker i-Saraan Plus EPF Contribution Deduction for Platform Providers' story covers the deduction engine; this story covers the KWSP monthly contribution report, annual matching-cap tracking (RM600/year per worker), and TP1 relief amount surfacing.
acceptanceCriteria: Gig Worker record includes 'i-Saraan Plus Voluntary Rate' field (worker-specified percentage or fixed amount per transaction, set by the worker)
Monthly i-Saraan Plus contribution report generated for KWSP i-Akaun upload: EPF number, NRIC, worker name, voluntary contribution amount per period
Platform provider dashboard shows: total i-Saraan Plus contributions facilitated per month and cumulative government matching eligibility per worker (capped at RM600/year)
Alert when a worker's annual RM600 matching cap is reached — voluntary contributions above cap still proceed but system notes no further matching available
i-Saraan Plus amounts reported in a separate section from any mandatory EPF contributions in the EPF i-Akaun CSV upload, using the correct EPF contribution type code
TP1 relief tracking: worker's total i-Saraan Plus voluntary contributions for the year displayed on their TP1 summary, supporting their RM4,000 voluntary EPF relief claim
technicalNotes: i-Saraan Plus: government matches up to RM600/year (RM6,000 lifetime) vs existing i-Saraan at RM500/year (RM5,000 lifetime)
Only gig workers registered under Act 872 via platform providers and who are EPF members qualify for i-Saraan Plus matching
Platform providers facilitate voluntary deductions from gig worker earnings at a rate chosen by the worker
KWSP policy enhancements 2026: https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/w/news/epf-policy-product-enhancements-2026
Existing story 'Implement Gig Worker i-Saraan Plus EPF Contribution Deduction for Platform Providers' covers the deduction calculation; this story covers the KWSP submission report and cap tracking
dependencies: Implement Gig Worker i-Saraan Plus EPF Contribution Deduction for Platform Providers
Generate EPF i-Akaun Electronic Upload File
